definition: Extent to which backup suppliers routes inventory capacity data systems and emergency alternatives exist
measurement_note: Use qualified supplier count route alternatives strategic inventory reserve capacity backup systems and spare-parts metrics

definition: Ability to shift sourcing production routing inventory allocation contracts and logistics under stress
measurement_note: Use reroute time supplier substitution time manufacturing flexibility intermodal options contract flexibility and allocation capability

definition: Ability to observe multi-tier suppliers shipment status inventory bottlenecks cyber conditions climate exposure and demand shifts
measurement_note: Use tier mapping real-time tracking data quality bottleneck detection forecast error and visibility coverage indicators

definition: Ability of firms suppliers logistics providers governments workers and public agencies to share information and align decisions
measurement_note: Use emergency coordination protocols data-sharing agreements allocation processes public-private coordination and response drills

definition: Ability to learn from disruption and redesign supply networks over time
measurement_note: Use stress tests after-action reviews updated sourcing policies supplier development scenario planning and governance revision indicators

definition: Extent to which resilience strategies protect workers small suppliers consumers communities and environmental justice
measurement_note: Use labor rights supplier finance affordability forced-labor due diligence environmental safeguards and community impact metrics

definition: Continuity and adaptation of ports roads rail warehouses cold chains energy water customs and digital systems
measurement_note: Use outage time infrastructure exposure maintenance backlog backup power route reliability and climate adaptation indicators

definition: Exposure to concentration chokepoints climate hazards cyber dependency geopolitical risk and cascading failure
measurement_note: Higher values should trigger exposure-reduction dependency-mapping climate-risk and cyber-risk review

definition: Administrative political legal technical financial and civic burden required to implement a strategy
measurement_note: Higher values should trigger feasibility review sequencing legal review financing strategy and governance design
